title: Control remote content and navigation
description: Restrict which URLs renderers may load, block unexpected navigation and new windows, and never grant Node to remote pages.
body: Use when an app loads any external URL or user-controlled links. Do not let a renderer navigate anywhere or open arbitrary new windows, and never load remote content into a Node-enabled renderer. Actions: handle will-navigate and setWindowOpenHandler to allow-list destinations, open external links in the system browser, set a strict Content-Security-Policy, and disable webview/webSecurity overrides. Validation signals include navigation to non-allow-listed URLs being blocked and external links opening in the OS browser. Risk boundary: uncontrolled navigation can load attacker content into your app's context and chain with any isolation weakness into RCE — treat navigation and window-open as a security surface to lock down.
